Text: It’s super  late, where’s Vera?
She left all my messages on read. That’s not normal for her.
Vera hasn’t posted on social media in 2 days, something just isn’t right.
Vera’s jacket is still on the coat rack, that means she didn’t even leave properly.
Her bag is still here,  her bed’s made,  she probably didn’t plan to be gone this long.
Empty… Vera always tossed her keys in here. That’s not like her.
Locked… that means she left. But why would she leave without her phone?
Wait! This has to be it!  Please let this be her room key.
Nothing useful here… I need to focus.
That looks out of place… why would she throw this away?
Her birthday’s circled. She never makes a big deal about it… Why mark it this time?
Of course. Vera and her obsession with strawberry pop tarts… she would devour an entire pack at once.
I can’t leave yet. Not until I figure out what happened.
Her birthday is circled on the calendar… and she loves TartPops. That has to be the password.
Yes! It worked. Let’s see what she’s been up to.
Something isn’t right. I have to follow this. First, the bar.
Let’s go. I need to find Vera and figure out what happened.
Nothing inside the cupboard… just the usual dishes.
Looks like the garbage can has already been taken out.
The counter is clear… maybe Vera left something behind?
The lamp is on… odd that it's still lit.
Her desk is a mess  but I don't see anything useful right now.
The shelf holds some books… nothing that seems important.
The exit door is locked… I can't go through here yet.
There's a framed photo on the wall… Vera looks happy in this.
Vera's computer is still on… maybe I can find a clue there.
The carpet is clean… nothing unusual to notice here.
A small brass key with faded enamel on the handle. A tag attached to it reads 'V' in messy handwriting. It looks worn from daily use.
A torn piece of paper, hastily crumpled and partially smoothed out. Some words are smudged, but it looks like Vera was writing something important before throwing it away.
An Apple iPhone 16 resting on the desk. The screen lights up but requires a word password. Several unread notifications are visible.
A small yellow sticky note stuck to the back of a drawer. It reads: 'Password = birthday + fav food.' The handwriting is unmistakably Vera’s.
